nameMeanings.name: Hereward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Harward
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hereward, usually representing Old English Hereweard (from here ‘army’ + weard ‘ward, guardian’) but possibly in some cases the cognate ancient Germanic Hariward, Heriward through Norman introduction. This name was borne by an 11th-century thane of Lincolnshire, leader of resistance to the advancing Normans.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Hereward was born in Saint Giles in the Wood, Devon, England. He had at least 1 son.
